{{wikidata link|Q38278497}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Musanimasu Shrine |画像=[[File:Musanimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Mize-chō 718 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 28.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 40.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|WD=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|WD=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むさにますしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Mize-chō 718 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 28.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 40.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|WD=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|WD=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q38278497}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Musanimasu Shrine |画像=[[File:Musanimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Mize-chō 718 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 28.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 40.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|WD=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|WD=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Mize-chō 718 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 28.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 40.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|WD=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|WD=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|WD=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|WD=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q38278497}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Musanimasu Shrine |画像=[[File:Musanimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Mize-chō 718 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 28.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 40.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|WD=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|WD=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|WD=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|WD=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|WD=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|WD=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|WD=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|WD=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|WD=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|WD=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|qid=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|qid=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|qid=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|qid=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|qid=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|qid=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|qid=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|qid=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|qid=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|qid=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|qid=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|qid=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|qid=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|qid=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|qid=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|qid=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|qid=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|qid=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|qid=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|qid=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}} era ({{ill|lt=454|Q30794|qid=Q30794|1=454}}-{{ill|lt=456|Q30432|qid=Q30432|1=456}}) |本殿=Ichiken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |地図= Japan Nara }} [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Musanimasu Jinja Shasou.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Shrine grove}}]] '''Musanimasu Shrine''' (むさにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}''' As older identifications of the ancient deity, the following appear: * {{ill|lt=Ameikutama-no-Mikoto|Q97184246|qid=Q97184246|lt=Ikudama-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'') * Ikazuchi-no-Kami (''Gogun Jinjaki'') === History === === Foundation === The origin is said to be enshrinement of Ikazuchi-no-Kami by the immigrant leader Misa no Suguri Ao in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankō|Q361010|qid=Q361010|1=Emperor Ankō}}. Misa (=Musa) was a place name in Takachi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, regarded as the base area of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|qid=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} who naturalized under {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|qid=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}}. Misa no Suguri Ao was a favored retainer of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Five Kings of Wa|Q1152880|qid=Q1152880|1=Five kings of Wa}}. ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' records missions he led to Wu/Song in Yūryaku 8 ({{ill|lt=464|Q30150|qid=Q30150|1=464}}) and Yūryaku 12 ({{ill|lt=468|Q30823|qid=Q30823|1=468}}), though these are not found in the ''{{ill|lt=Book of Song|Q1143142|qid=Q1143142|1=Book of Song}}''. === Historical outline === According to ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|qid=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, in Takachi District, the deity “Kotoshironushi at {{ill|lt=Kawamata Shrine|Q11553360|qid=Q11553360|lt=Takachi Shrine|1=Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}} and Ikudama-no-Kami at Misa Shrine” possessed {{ill|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|Q11669864|qid=Q11669864|lt=Takachi Agatanushi Kome|1=Takechi no Kome}} and gave an {{ill|lt=oracle|Q217123|qid=Q217123|1=Oracle}}: “Offer horses and various weapons at the tomb of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu#mausoleum|Q200188|qid=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko|1=Emperor Jimmu}},” and then Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) would be protected.''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 28 After his enthronement, divine rank was granted to Misa-ni-masu Ikudama-no-Kami for the first time in history. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a great shrine and recorded as receiving offerings for monthly and Niiname rites.
Its former rank was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - written as “Musanimasu-no-Kami.” === Access === * Just west on foot from {{ill|lt=Okadera Station|Q7081721|qid=Q7081721|1=Okadera Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Annotations === === Related items === {{Commonscat|Musanimasu-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|qid=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|qid=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22813 Musanimasu Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Shinto/Shrine Materials Collection" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Musanimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]
